tomato__bacterial_spot: Rotate crops (avoid planting tomatoes in the same location for 2-3 years)
Ensure proper spacing between plants for good air circulation
tomato__early_blight: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper
Mulch around the base of plants
Water at soil level rather than on foliage
tomato__late_blight: Apply fungicides proactively before symptoms appear
tomato__leaf_mold: Increase spacing between plants to improve air circulation
Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper
Keep foliage dry by watering at the base
tomato__septoria_leaf_spot: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper
Mulch around plants to prevent spores splashing from soil
tomato__spider_mites_(two_spotted_spider: Spray plants with strong streams of water to dislodge mites
Apply insecticidal soap or neem oil
tomato__yellow_leaf_curl_virus: No cure available - remove and destroy infected plants
Use reflective mulches to repel whiteflies
tomato__mosaic_virus: No cure available - remove and destroy infected plants
Wash hands and tools after handling infected plants
potato__early_blight: Ensure proper hilling to protect tubers
potato__late_blight: Ensure proper storage conditions for harvested potatoes

DEFAULT_HEALTHY_PRACTICES: Monitor regularly for signs of disease
